page: Amazon Web Services c6i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. This configuration delivers 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The processor includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 54 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for AVX-512 vector extensions and cryptography acceleration. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results place the c6i.8xlarge in the average performance tier for most standard workloads. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1664.0 and a multi-core score of 13945.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 36326.98. Memory bandwidth tests show 147.98 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 66.82 GB/sec for write operations. In specialized tasks, the instance demonstrates strong performance in text generation for small LLM models (135M parameters) at 303.91 tokens/sec, though performance drops to a weak 6.2 tokens/sec for prompt processing on large 70B models. Redis SET operations reach 31.19 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is measured at 35.25 GB/sec.

The c6i.8xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, owing to its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy operations. Network bandwidth is baseline-rated at 12 Gbps. This instance is appropriate for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, web serving, and small-to-medium scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c6i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by a dedicated 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 2.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average tier performance across standard compute metrics, with a Passmark CPU score of 36326.98 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 13945.0. It exhibits strong performance in text generation for small 135M LLM models at 303.91 tokens/sec, but drops to a weak 6.2 tokens/sec for large 70B models. This hardware profile is suited for CPU-bound tasks like software compilation, web serving, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering balanced performance for CPU-bound workloads.

tagline: A dedicated 32-vCPU compute-optimized instance featuring Intel Xeon processors and DDR4 memory for high-density CPU workloads.

64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per core ratio
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU accelerators
